title: Titles, Canonicals, Robots Directives, Hreflang, and Structured Metadata · Type Forensics
summary: A preserved TypeScript crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, Fetch, stream, Cheerio, selector, frontier, Promise, browser, axe, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in evidence-linked indexing and metadata audit and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Audit title and description presence, uniqueness, length evidence, visible consistency, and stakeholder intent without ranking promises.
Resolve canonical links and reconcile redirects, sitemap URLs, internal links, content identity, and scope before reporting conflicts.
Parse robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by agent, precedence, response, and scope while keeping crawl and index policy distinct.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Title Description
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 2611 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile title, canonical, robots directives, hreflang graphs, JSON-LD, and explicit search non-claims; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Audit title and description presence, uniqueness, length evidence, visible consistency, and stakeholder intent without ranking promises.

Inspect the Title Description trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Title Description.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for audit title and description presence, uniqueness, length evidence, visible consistency, and stakeholder intent without ranking promises. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Title Description, keep Title Description connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: The Title Description record says evidence-linked indexing and metadata audit looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Title Description.

For inspect the failure record, fetch one convenient page, omit owner authorization, source and emitted revision, URL and robots policy, runtime admission, stream and Promise ownership, Cheerio and browser differences, accessibility states, changed fixtures, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as TypeScript crawler proof. This does not satisfy Title Description for evidence-linked indexing and metadata audit.
The record names the assumption, applies Title Description, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Jsonld Admission
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-jsonld-admission-1apk6r" that uses case 6226 to reconcile title, canonical, robots directives, hreflang graphs, JSON-LD, and explicit search non-claims. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Jsonld Admission.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Jsonld Admission into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Jsonld Admission, keep Jsonld Admission connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Jsonld Admission structural check is only one gate. evidence-linked indexing and metadata audit must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
